marker_contract: Every substantive section-level claim is followed by an HTML comment of the form <!-- claim: XA-NN -->.

claims.summary: The useful unit of analysis is a governed stack with replaceable cognitive substrates, explicit authority, evidence, readiness, and rollback.
claims.boundary: Does not establish an implemented, safe, generally superior, AGI, or ASI system.

claims.summary: Cognition, authority, verification, release, and observed effects should be separate system responsibilities.
claims.boundary: Finite boundary records and local effects do not prove hidden-effect exclusion or deployed control.

claims.summary: Architectural self-improvement must requalify new substrates rather than inherit permissions from replaced components.
claims.boundary: No autonomous recursive self-improvement, safe architecture search, or general capability gain is established.

claims.summary: Intent fields need provenance so inferred meaning cannot silently become authority.
claims.boundary: Does not prove natural-language intent understanding, legitimacy, or open-world ambiguity resolution.

claims.summary: Planning can be treated as typed compilation with inspectable obligations and downstream invalidation.
claims.boundary: No complete semantic parser, arbitrary graph proof, or production compiler correctness is established.

claims.summary: Context should expose version, provenance, permitted use, taint, and typed resolution faults through a stable ABI.
claims.boundary: Does not establish a deployed memory store, semantic summary fidelity, privacy, or complete descendant deletion.

claims.summary: Important claims should carry scope, sources, evidence lane, falsifier, dependencies, support state, and counterevidence.
claims.boundary: A typed ledger improves calibration but does not make claims true or evaluators reliable.

claims.summary: Proof-carrying AI should mean a bounded consumer-specific proof envelope, not a universal proof of output truth.
claims.boundary: Formal correctness is limited to the model and assumptions and does not prove observation, authority, or system safety.

claims.summary: A consequential action needs a trace from scoped grant through request, observed effect, and terminal closure.
claims.boundary: Local trace coverage does not prove production authorization, distributed execution, or hidden-effect exclusion.

claims.summary: Exact rollback, compensation, quarantine, and acknowledged irreversibility are different terminal outcomes.
claims.boundary: No effect-complete rollback across arbitrary external descendants is established.

claims.summary: Capability fields let providers qualify, degrade, quarantine, recover, and retire independently of model identity.
claims.boundary: Declared qualification records do not establish real capability, benchmark adequacy, or safe replacement.

claims.summary: Useful routing requires ambiguous workloads, meaningful route differences, fallback, abstention, and joint safety/usefulness scoring.
claims.boundary: The mixed local result, including two unsafe full-policy outputs, supports no general superiority or core promotion.

claims.summary: Deliberation needs stopping, budget, evaluator separation, safety gates, and explicit accounting for fifteen known harms.
claims.boundary: No universal benefit, calibrated rare-risk estimate, or safe deliberation policy is established.

claims.summary: Efficiency should measure useful governed work jointly with unsafe release, latency, compute, review, and residual cost.
claims.boundary: The bounded local operating point is not transfer, external reproduction, or general efficiency dominance.

claims.summary: Behavioral unlearning, causal influence, membership privacy, lineage, legal deletion, storage erasure, backups, and external descendants are distinct axes.
claims.boundary: The broad unlearning interpretation was narrowed; total storage erasure and external-descendant closure were false.

claims.summary: Situated world models and memory consolidation should preserve observation-to-belief provenance and compression losses.
claims.boundary: The finite synthetic result does not establish open-world understanding, skill discovery, or transfer.

claims.summary: Transformers, state-space and recurrent systems, KANs, tree and symbolic systems, search, and conventional code can share the same capability contracts.
claims.boundary: No architecture family was locally reproduced as generally dominant or SOTA.

claims.summary: Compressed artifacts should bind sources, transformation, fidelity target, verifier, losses, residuals, and consumers.
claims.boundary: No general semantic-preservation, compression-quality, or transfer claim follows.

claims.summary: Safety cases should be living dependency graphs that can be invalidated by changed evidence, controls, models, or incidents.
claims.boundary: Inspectable assurance structure is not a completed safety case or proof of system safety.

claims.summary: The current evidence ledger has 3,745 atoms: 3,698 blocked, 36 retained, nine narrowed, two bounded subordinate promotions, and all 55 cores at argument.
claims.boundary: Coverage and disposition counts are calibration metadata, not evidence that the architecture works.

claims.summary: A bounded local reference slice composes intent, planning, context, claims, routing, execution, effects, evidence, release, and closure.
claims.boundary: The local slice is not distributed, production-secure, generally capable, or transfer evidence.

claims.summary: A practical adoption path starts with proposal/permission separation, claim records, readiness, honest rollback, and evidence-lane labels.
claims.boundary: The proposed sequence is a design recommendation, not an empirical claim of optimal adoption order.

claims.summary: The main unfinished work is external reproduction and transfer, real effect-complete systems, calibrated evaluators, full-state updates, and semantically deep refinement.
claims.boundary: These are open obligations, not evidence that the missing capabilities will be achieved.

claims.summary: A living book should bind releases to exact evidence and visibly supersede stale claims and derivatives.
claims.boundary: A live maintenance process does not guarantee claim truth, freshness between releases, or publication quality.
